articles.headline: India Unveils 'National Climate Resilience Mission' to Fortify Infrastructure and Livelihoods
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet today approved the launch of the National Climate Resilience Mission (NCRM) as a flagship initiative under the 'Viksit Bharat 2047' vision.
• NCRM aims to integrate climate adaptation and mitigation strategies across 12 key sectors, including agriculture, water resources, urban development, and coastal management.
• The mission will focus on developing climate-proof infrastructure, promoting climate-smart agricultural practices, and enhancing early warning systems.
• A substantial initial outlay of ₹1.5 lakh crore has been sanctioned for the first five years, with significant private sector participation encouraged through incentive schemes.
• It emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach, involving central and state governments, local bodies, scientific institutions, and community organizations.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: National Climate Resilience Mission (NCRM) launched.
NCRM is a flagship initiative under 'Viksit Bharat 2047'.
Initial outlay: ₹1.5 lakh crore for 5 years.
Focus sectors include agriculture, water resources, urban development, coastal management.
Emphasizes multi-stakeholder approach.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of NCRM in achieving India's climate goals and sustainable development.
Analyze the multi-sectoral approach of NCRM and its potential impact on various economic and social segments.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships and community involvement in the successful implementation of NCRM.
Critically evaluate the challenges and opportunities in climate-proofing India's infrastructure and livelihoods.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of climate-resilient infrastructure like green buildings and flood barriers, symbolizing adaptation to climate change.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top 10 countries most vulnerable to climate change impacts, underscoring the urgency of such missions.

articles.headline: RBI Keeps Repo Rate Unchanged at 6.5% Citing Persistent Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) unanimously decided to maintain the repo rate at 6.50% in its latest review.
• This marks the sixth consecutive time the central bank has held the key lending rate steady, prioritizing inflation control over growth stimulus.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted global uncertainties and domestic food price volatility as key factors influencing the decision.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the 4% target within a flexible band.
• Projections for Q3 FY25 show retail inflation moderating but remaining above the comfort zone, while GDP growth forecasts remain robust.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC maintained repo rate at 6.50%.
Sixth consecutive time the rate has been held steady.
Reason: Persistent inflationary pressures, particularly food inflation.
RBI's primary mandate is inflation control (4% target).
Stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate, considering its dual mandate of inflation and growth.
Discuss the implications of a sustained high repo rate on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending, investment, and consumption.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy in managing supply-side inflation, particularly food inflation, in India.
Examine the challenges faced by RBI in balancing global economic uncertainties with domestic growth imperatives.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai, symbolizing monetary policy decisions.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India-France Joint Naval Exercise 'Varuna-2024' Concludes, Enhancing Maritime Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The 22nd edition of the bilateral naval exercise 'Varuna' between the Indian Navy and the French Navy successfully concluded in the Arabian Sea.
• The exercise saw advanced air defence drills, anti-submarine warfare operations, tactical manoeuvres, and live firing exercises.
• Participation included frontline destroyers, frigates, submarines, maritime patrol aircraft, and helicopters from both navies.
• Varuna-2024 aimed to enhance interoperability, share best practices, and strengthen maritime security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• The exercise underscores the growing strategic partnership between India and France, particularly in defense and security domains.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Varuna-2024 is a bilateral naval exercise between India and France.
It was the 22nd edition of the exercise.
Concluded in the Arabian Sea.
Focus on air defence, anti-submarine warfare, tactical manoeuvres.
Aims to enhance interoperability and maritime security in Indo-Pacific.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of the Varuna exercise in the context of India's 'Act East' policy and its engagement in the Indo-Pacific.
Analyze how such bilateral exercises contribute to strengthening India's defense capabilities and regional security architecture.
Examine the broader implications of India-France strategic partnership for global geopolitics and multilateral cooperation.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in forging defense alliances with European powers.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and French naval ships conducting joint manoeuvres at sea during the Varuna exercise.
articles.trivia: The 'Varuna' exercise was initiated in 1993 and was christened 'Varuna' in 2001, signifying its importance to both navies.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Mandate for Digitization of Land Records, Stresses Transparency
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today dismissed a batch of petitions challenging the government's mandate for complete digitization of land records across the country.
• A bench headed by Chief Justice of India emphasized that digitization is crucial for reducing land disputes, preventing fraudulent transactions, and enhancing transparency.
• The court directed all states and Union Territories to expedite the digitization process under the Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP).
• It also mandated the establishment of robust grievance redressal mechanisms for citizens to address discrepancies in digitized records.
• The judgment is expected to streamline land administration, improve ease of doing business, and protect property rights more effectively.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary.
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential.
keyTerms: Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP)
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld mandate for land record digitization.
Digitization under Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP).
Aims to reduce land disputes and fraud.
Mandated robust grievance redressal mechanisms.
Focus on transparency and property rights protection.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of land record digitization for governance, economic development, and social justice in India.
Analyze the challenges encountered in the implementation of the Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP) and potential solutions.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in driving administrative reforms and ensuring transparency in public services.
Examine the impact of digitized land records on ease of doing business and attracting investments in various sectors.
articles.imageDescription: A digital tablet displaying land records overlaid on a map, symbolizing e-governance in land administration.
articles.trivia: The DILRMP was launched in 2008 by merging two schemes: the Computerization of Land Records (CLR) and the Strengthening of Revenue Administration and Updation of Land Records (SRA&ULR).

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests New Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle Engine for Future Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful static fire test of a new generation 'semi-cryogenic engine' for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program.
• This engine, designed for high thrust and efficiency, is a critical component for India's ambition to develop cost-effective space access.
• The test was conducted at ISRO Propulsion Complex (IPRC) in Mahendragiri, Tamil Nadu, for a sustained duration.
• The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) project aims to achieve two-stage-to-orbit capability, significantly reducing launch costs and turnaround time.
• This development brings India closer to its goal of developing fully reusable launch vehicles, similar to global space majors.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested a new generation semi-cryogenic engine.
Engine is for the Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program.
Test conducted at IPRC, Mahendragiri.
RLV-TD aims for two-stage-to-orbit capability.
Goal: reduce launch costs and turnaround time.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of reusable launch vehicle technology for India's future space missions and its position in the global space market.
Analyze the economic and strategic advantages of developing indigenous semi-cryogenic engines for space propulsion.
Examine the challenges and advancements required to achieve full reusability in launch vehicles, drawing parallels with international efforts.
Evaluate how ISRO's R&D in advanced propulsion systems contributes to India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision in space technology.
articles.imageDescription: A powerful jet of flame emanating from a rocket engine during a static fire test at an ISRO facility.
articles.trivia: ISRO's RLV-TD program successfully completed its first experimental test flight, RLV-TD HEX (Hypersonic Flight Experiment), in 2016, demonstrating critical technologies.

articles.headline: Draft National Policy on Elderly Care Released, Focuses on Holistic Well-being and Digital Inclusion
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment today released the draft National Policy on Elderly Care for public feedback.
• The policy aims to create an age-friendly society by ensuring financial security, healthcare, housing, and protection against abuse for senior citizens.
• A key focus is on leveraging digital technology to provide accessible services, including telemedicine, online grievance redressal, and digital literacy programs for the elderly.
• It proposes strengthening family support systems and community-based care, along with promoting active and healthy aging.
• The draft policy highlights the need for dedicated infrastructure and trained professionals to cater to the growing demographic of the elderly in India.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
Population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
articles.source: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
prelimsPoints: Draft National Policy on Elderly Care released by Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment.
Aims for age-friendly society, financial security, healthcare, protection.
Focus on digital technology for services (telemedicine, online grievance).
Promotes family and community-based care.
Addresses growing elderly demographic in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the demographic challenges posed by an aging population in India and the necessity of a comprehensive elderly care policy.
Analyze how the proposed policy addresses the multi-dimensional needs of senior citizens, including health, financial security, and social inclusion.
Evaluate the role of digital technology in enhancing the accessibility and quality of services for the elderly as envisioned in the draft policy.
Critically examine the balance between institutional care and family/community-based support systems in the context of Indian societal values.
articles.imageDescription: An elderly couple using a tablet device, symbolizing digital inclusion and connectivity for senior citizens.
articles.trivia: As per the 2011 Census, the population of senior citizens in India was 10.38 crore, projected to increase significantly by 2050.

articles.headline: PM Inaugurates Phase II of Bharatmala Pariyojana, Focus on Economic Corridors and Logistics Efficiency
articles.explainer:
• Prime Minister Narendra Modi today virtually inaugurated the second phase of the Bharatmala Pariyojana, a massive road infrastructure program.
• Phase II focuses on developing an additional 8,000 km of economic corridors, inter-corridor and feeder routes, and coastal and port connectivity roads.
• The project aims to enhance logistics efficiency, reduce travel time, and integrate remote regions into the national economic mainstream.
• Special emphasis is placed on leveraging technology for faster project execution and ensuring environmental sustainability.
• This phase is expected to generate significant employment opportunities and boost regional economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: Phase II of Bharatmala Pariyojana inaugurated.
Aims to develop 8,000 km of economic corridors, feeder routes.
Focus on logistics efficiency, reduced travel time.
Emphasizes technology and environmental sustainability.
Expected to boost employment and regional growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Bharatmala Pariyojana in transforming India's road infrastructure and its contribution to economic development.
Analyze how the focus on economic corridors and logistics efficiency in Phase II will impact trade, industry, and regional connectivity.
Evaluate the challenges associated with large-scale infrastructure projects like Bharatmala, including land acquisition, environmental clearances, and funding.
Examine the role of infrastructure development in achieving the 'Viksit Bharat' vision and enhancing India's competitiveness.
articles.imageDescription: A modern six-lane highway stretching across a landscape, symbolizing advanced road infrastructure.
articles.trivia: The Bharatmala Pariyojana was launched in 2017 with an initial outlay of ₹5.35 lakh crore for Phase I, aiming to construct 34,800 km of roads.

articles.headline: India Calls for Global Framework on AI Governance at UN General Assembly
articles.explainer:
• India's Permanent Representative to the UN urged member states to collaboratively develop a global framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance.
• The call was made during a high-level debate at the UN General Assembly on the ethical and responsible use of AI.
• India emphasized the need for an inclusive framework that addresses issues of bias, data privacy, security, and the equitable distribution of AI benefits.
• It highlighted the potential of AI for sustainable development but cautioned against its misuse, especially in critical sectors.
• India reaffirmed its commitment to promoting human-centric AI development and fostering international cooperation on AI ethics.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India called for a global AI governance framework at UN General Assembly.
Focus on ethical and responsible use of AI.
Addresses bias, data privacy, security, equitable benefits.
Highlights AI's potential for sustainable development.
India promotes human-centric AI.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's stance on global AI governance and its implications for international cooperation and technological development.
Analyze the ethical challenges associated with the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence and the need for a multilateral regulatory framework.
Evaluate the potential benefits and risks of AI in the context of sustainable development goals and human rights.
Examine how a global AI governance framework could address issues of digital divide and ensure equitable access to AI technologies for developing nations.
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations emblem against a backdrop of digital code, symbolizing global efforts in AI governance.
articles.trivia: India is a founding member of the Global Partnership on Artificial Intelligence (GPAI), an international initiative to bridge the gap between theory and practice on AI.

articles.headline: New Policy Framework to Boost Indigenous Defence Manufacturing and Exports
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence today announced a new policy framework aimed at significantly boosting indigenous defence manufacturing and exports.
• The policy introduces enhanced incentives for private sector participation, including MSMEs and startups, in defence production.
• It streamlines procurement procedures, emphasizes 'Design and Development' in India, and promotes technology transfer within the domestic industry.
• The framework sets ambitious targets for self-reliance in critical defence technologies and aims to position India as a net defence exporter.
• Measures include a revised 'Make in India' clause for defence acquisition and increased allocation for R&D in defence startups.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Defence.
prelimsPoints: New policy framework for indigenous defence manufacturing announced.
Boosts private sector, MSMEs, startups in defence production.
Streamlines procurement, emphasizes 'Design and Development' in India.
Aims for self-reliance in critical defence technologies.
India targets becoming a net defence exporter.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic imperative for India to achieve self-reliance in defence manufacturing and its implications for national security.
Analyze the key provisions of the new policy framework and how they are expected to boost indigenous production and exports.
Evaluate the challenges faced by India's defence industrial complex and the role of private sector participation in overcoming them.
Examine the potential economic benefits and job creation opportunities arising from a robust domestic defence manufacturing ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: A modern fighter jet being assembled in an Indian factory, symbolizing indigenous defence production.
articles.trivia: India has been among the world's largest importers of defence equipment, a trend the new policy aims to reverse significantly.

articles.headline: Union Minister Launches Integrated 'EIA-Plus' Portal for Streamlined Environmental Clearances
articles.explainer:
• The Union Minister for Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched the 'EIA-Plus' portal, an integrated online system for environmental clearances.
• The new portal aims to bring greater transparency, efficiency, and accountability to the environmental impact assessment (EIA) process.
• It incorporates features like real-time tracking of applications, automated compliance monitoring, and a public access module for project information.
• The move is part of the government's broader agenda to improve ease of doing business while upholding environmental safeguards.
• Stakeholders expect the portal to reduce delays and ensure timely project approvals with stringent environmental oversight.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: EIA-Plus portal launched for environmental clearances.
Launched by Union Minister for Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Aims for transparency, efficiency, accountability in EIA.
Features real-time tracking, automated compliance, public access.
Part of 'ease of doing business' initiative with environmental safeguards.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the 'EIA-Plus' portal in reforming India's environmental clearance process and promoting sustainable development.
Analyze how digital platforms like EIA-Plus can enhance transparency and reduce corruption in regulatory mechanisms.
Evaluate the potential impact of streamlined environmental clearances on industrial growth and investment, while ensuring ecological integrity.
Critically examine the balance between environmental protection and developmental needs in India's policy framework.
articles.imageDescription: A laptop screen displaying an online portal with environmental data and clearance status, symbolizing digital governance in environment.
articles.trivia: The concept of Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) was introduced in India in 1978 with respect to river valley projects and formally established under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.

articles.headline: Report Highlights India's Progress and Potential in Green Hydrogen Economy
articles.explainer:
• A new report by NITI Aayog and the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy outlines India's significant strides in developing a green hydrogen ecosystem.
• The report emphasizes the potential for India to become a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, leveraging its renewable energy resources.
• It details policy interventions, technological advancements, and investment opportunities under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• Challenges such as high production costs, infrastructure development, and demand creation are also addressed with proposed solutions.
• The findings underscore green hydrogen's crucial role in decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors and achieving India's net-zero targets.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Report by NITI Aayog and Ministry of New and Renewable Energy on green hydrogen.
India's potential as a global hub for green hydrogen production.
Outlines policy interventions under National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Addresses challenges like high production costs, infrastructure.
Green hydrogen crucial for decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security, economic growth, and climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the key policy measures and incentives under the National Green Hydrogen Mission aimed at fostering a robust green hydrogen ecosystem.
Evaluate the technological and infrastructural challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Examine the potential of green hydrogen in decarbonizing critical industrial sectors and its role in achieving India's net-zero emissions target by 2070.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized illustration of hydrogen molecules being produced from renewable energy sources like wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy, distinguishing it from grey hydrogen (from fossil fuels) and blue hydrogen (from fossil fuels with carbon capture).

articles.headline: Centre Issues New Guidelines for Enhanced Financial Autonomy of Urban Local Bodies
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs today released comprehensive guidelines aimed at strengthening the financial autonomy and sustainability of Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
• The guidelines encourage ULBs to explore innovative revenue generation mechanisms, including municipal bonds, property tax reforms, and user charges for services.
• They also propose capacity building for ULB officials in financial management, project planning, and public-private partnerships.
• The move is intended to reduce ULBs' dependence on central and state grants and empower them to effectively manage urban development initiatives.
• This initiative aligns with the government's vision for 'Smart Cities' and decentralized governance, promoting local self-reliance.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: New guidelines for financial autonomy of Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) released.
Issued by Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
Encourages ULBs to use municipal bonds, property tax reforms, user charges.
Aims to reduce dependence on central/state grants.
Aligns with 'Smart Cities' and decentralized governance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in India regarding financial resources and their impact on urban governance and service delivery.
Analyze how the new guidelines promote financial autonomy and sustainability for ULBs, and their potential to improve urban infrastructure and services.
Evaluate the role of innovative financing mechanisms like municipal bonds in mobilizing resources for urban development projects.
Critically examine the implications of enhanced financial autonomy for ULBs on the federal structure and decentralized planning in India.
articles.imageDescription: A modern city skyline with a graph showing increasing revenue, symbolizing urban financial growth and autonomy.
articles.trivia: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, gave constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies, defining their powers, functions, and financial provisions.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Policy to Boost Millets Cultivation and Consumption, Promote Nutritional Security
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare today unveiled a new national policy aimed at promoting the cultivation, processing, and consumption of millets.
• The policy seeks to enhance nutritional security, support small and marginal farmers, and diversify agricultural practices.
• Key initiatives include providing financial incentives for millet farmers, establishing millet-processing units, and integrating millets into public distribution systems.
• It also focuses on awareness campaigns to highlight the health benefits of millets and boost their demand in urban areas.
• This move aligns with India's leadership in declaring 2023 as the 'International Year of Millets' and promoting them as 'Shree Anna'.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: New national policy on millets launched by Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare.
Aims to promote cultivation, processing, consumption of millets.
Focus on nutritional security, support to small farmers, agricultural diversification.
Incentives for farmers, millet processing units, PDS integration.
Aligns with 'International Year of Millets' (2023) and 'Shree Anna'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of millets for nutritional security, climate resilience, and sustainable agriculture in India.
Analyze the key provisions of the new policy and their potential impact on farmers' income, food diversity, and public health.
Evaluate the challenges in mainstreaming millets into the dietary habits of the Indian population and the role of awareness campaigns.
Examine how promoting millets aligns with India's broader agricultural policy goals and its commitment to sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: Various types of millets like jowar, bajra, and ragi displayed in bowls, symbolizing their nutritional value.
articles.trivia: India is the largest producer of millets in the world, accounting for about 41% of global millet production.

articles.headline: Ganga River Dolphin Census Shows Promising Increase in Population, Conservation Efforts Lauded
articles.explainer:
• The latest census conducted by the Wildlife Institute of India (WII) and state forest departments indicates a notable increase in the population of the endangered Ganga River Dolphin.
• The census, spanning across major stretches of the Ganga and its tributaries, attributes the rise to improved water quality and sustained conservation initiatives.
• Measures like reduced poaching, habitat protection, and community engagement programs have played a crucial role in the species' recovery.
• The Ganga River Dolphin, India's National Aquatic Animal, is an indicator species for the health of the river ecosystem.
• Despite the positive trend, challenges such as plastic pollution, dam construction, and accidental entanglement in fishing nets persist, requiring continued vigilance.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity.
prelimsPoints: Ganga River Dolphin census shows population increase.
Conducted by WII and state forest departments.
Increase attributed to improved water quality, conservation.
Ganga River Dolphin is India's National Aquatic Animal.
Challenges: plastic pollution, dams, fishing nets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Ganga River Dolphin as an indicator species and the factors contributing to its population increase.
Analyze the effectiveness of government-led conservation programs and community engagement in protecting endangered aquatic species.
Evaluate the persistent threats to the Ganga River Dolphin and propose sustainable solutions for long-term conservation.
Examine the broader implications of improved river health for biodiversity and the livelihoods dependent on the Ganga ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: A Ganga River Dolphin surfacing in a clear river, symbolizing successful conservation efforts.
articles.trivia: The Ganga River Dolphin is one of only four freshwater dolphin species in the world, and it is functionally blind, relying on echolocation to navigate and hunt.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Policy on Critical Minerals to Boost Domestic Supply Chain
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Mines today unveiled a new policy framework for critical minerals, aimed at securing a robust domestic supply chain and reducing import dependence.
• The policy identifies 30 critical minerals essential for India's economic development, national security, and transition to a green economy.
• Key provisions include incentivizing exploration, streamlining auction processes for mining leases, and promoting advanced processing technologies.
• It also emphasizes strategic partnerships with international suppliers and investments in overseas critical mineral assets.
• The move is crucial for industries like electric vehicles, renewable energy, defense, and high-tech electronics.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: New policy framework on critical minerals announced by Ministry of Mines.
Identifies 30 critical minerals for economic development, national security, green economy.
Incentivizes exploration, streamlines auction, promotes processing tech.
Emphasizes strategic international partnerships.
Crucial for EVs, renewable energy, defence, high-tech electronics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of critical minerals for India's economic growth, technological advancement, and national security.
Analyze the key features of the new policy framework on critical minerals and its potential to strengthen India's domestic supply chain.
Evaluate the challenges in sourcing and processing critical minerals in India and the role of international collaborations.
Examine how this policy supports India's transition to a green economy and its 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision in strategic sectors.
articles.imageDescription: Various samples of critical minerals like lithium, cobalt, and rare earth elements, highlighting their importance for modern technology.
articles.trivia: Critical minerals are those that are essential for modern technologies and economic growth, but whose supply chains are vulnerable to disruption.

articles.headline: Swachh Bharat Mission-Urban 2.0 Reports Significant Progress in Waste Management and Sanitation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs today released a progress report on Swachh Bharat Mission-Urban (SBM-U) 2.0, highlighting significant achievements.
• The report indicates substantial progress in solid waste management, with increased waste processing capacity and reduction in landfills.
• Urban areas have achieved nearly 100% door-to-door waste collection and improved sanitation infrastructure, including public and community toilets.
• The mission has also focused on promoting a circular economy approach through waste segregation, recycling, and waste-to-wealth initiatives.
• Challenges remain in behavioral change and sustainable financing, but the overall trajectory points towards cleaner and healthier cities.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: SBM-U 2.0 progress report released by Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
Significant progress in solid waste management, increased processing capacity.
Nearly 100% door-to-door waste collection in urban areas.
Focus on circular economy, waste segregation, recycling.
Challenges: behavioral change, sustainable financing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and achievements of Swachh Bharat Mission-Urban 2.0 in transforming urban sanitation and waste management in India.
Analyze the concept of circular economy in waste management and its role in achieving sustainable urban development.
Evaluate the challenges in sustaining the gains of SBM-U 2.0, particularly concerning behavioral change and financial viability of ULBs.
Examine the socio-economic impact of improved sanitation and waste management on public health and quality of life in Indian cities.
articles.imageDescription: A clean urban street with separate bins for waste segregation, symbolizing effective waste management.
articles.trivia: Swachh Bharat Mission (SBM) was launched on October 2, 2014, to achieve universal sanitation coverage and eliminate open defecation.

articles.headline: Ministry of Health Announces New Policy to Boost India's Medical Tourism Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare today announced a new national policy aimed at positioning India as a global hub for medical tourism.
• The policy focuses on enhancing infrastructure, ensuring quality healthcare services, and promoting traditional Indian systems of medicine (AYUSH).
• Key initiatives include developing a dedicated portal for medical tourists, streamlining visa processes, and establishing specialized medical tourism zones.
• It also emphasizes training healthcare professionals in hospitality and cross-cultural communication to cater to international patients.
• The move is expected to boost foreign exchange earnings, create employment, and showcase India's healthcare capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Investment models.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life (Health).
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: New national policy on medical tourism announced by Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
Aims to position India as a global medical tourism hub.
Focus on infrastructure, quality healthcare, AYUSH systems.
Initiatives: dedicated portal, streamlined visas, specialized zones.
Expected to boost foreign exchange, employment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of medical tourism in India and its contribution to economic growth and healthcare sector development.
Analyze the key provisions of the new policy and how they address existing challenges in attracting international patients.
Evaluate the role of traditional Indian systems of medicine (AYUSH) in promoting medical tourism and their integration with modern healthcare.
Examine the ethical considerations and regulatory frameworks required to ensure high-quality and responsible medical tourism practices in India.
articles.imageDescription: A diverse group of international patients interacting with doctors in a modern Indian hospital, symbolizing medical tourism.
articles.trivia: India's medical tourism sector is projected to grow significantly, driven by cost-effectiveness, quality treatment, and the availability of skilled healthcare professionals.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Habitation Sites in Rajasthan
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) today announced the discovery of several ancient habitation sites during an excavation in the Thar Desert region of Rajasthan.
• Preliminary findings suggest the sites date back to the pre-Harappan and early Harappan periods, providing new insights into early human settlements.
• Artefacts recovered include pottery shards, tools, and remnants of structural foundations, indicating a well-developed ancient culture.
• The discoveries challenge previous notions about the extent of early civilization in the region and highlight the adaptability of ancient communities.
• Further research is expected to shed light on the trade routes, agricultural practices, and socio-economic structures of these ancient inhabitants.
gsSubTopics: Indian Heritage and Culture, History and Geography of the World and Society.
Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI discovered ancient habitation sites in Thar Desert, Rajasthan.
Sites date back to pre-Harappan and early Harappan periods.
Artefacts: pottery, tools, structural remnants.
New insights into early human settlements and culture.
Further research on trade, agriculture, socio-economic structures.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of new archaeological discoveries in understanding the evolution of early civilizations in India, particularly in regions like the Thar Desert.
Analyze how such findings contribute to rewriting historical narratives and challenging existing archaeological theories about the Harappan civilization.
Evaluate the methods and challenges involved in archaeological excavations and the importance of preserving ancient heritage sites.
Examine the potential connections between these newly discovered sites and other major Harappan centers, shedding light on their cultural and economic interactions.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating a site in a desert landscape, revealing ancient pottery and tools.
articles.trivia: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who became its first Director-General.

articles.headline: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports Unveils Policy to Promote Indigenous Games and Traditional Sports
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports today launched a new national policy aimed at promoting and preserving India's indigenous games and traditional sports.
• The policy seeks to integrate these games into school curricula, establish regional training centers, and organize national-level competitions.
• It emphasizes the health benefits, cultural significance, and potential for rural employment generation through these sports.
• Initiatives include financial support for local sports clubs, documentation of traditional game rules, and digital promotion campaigns.
• The move is part of a broader effort to celebrate India's rich cultural heritage and foster a sense of national pride in traditional sports.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
articles.source: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports
prelimsPoints: New national policy to promote indigenous games and traditional sports launched.
By Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports.
Aims to integrate games into school curricula, establish training centers.
Emphasizes health benefits, cultural significance, rural employment.
Initiatives: financial support, documentation, digital promotion.
mainsPoints: Discuss the cultural and social significance of indigenous games and traditional sports in India's diverse heritage.
Analyze how the new policy aims to revive and popularize these games, contributing to physical fitness and national identity.
Evaluate the challenges in integrating traditional sports into modern education systems and professional sports frameworks.
Examine the potential of indigenous games to generate employment and promote local economies, particularly in rural areas.
articles.imageDescription: Children playing a traditional Indian game like Kabaddi or Kho Kho in a rural setting, symbolizing cultural preservation.
articles.trivia: Many traditional Indian games, like Chess (Chaturanga), originated in ancient India and have evolved over centuries.

highlights: India launches 'National Climate Resilience Mission' with ₹1.5 lakh crore outlay to climate-proof infrastructure and livelihoods.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5% for sixth consecutive time, prioritizing inflation control amidst global uncertainties.
India-France naval exercise 'Varuna-2024' concludes, enhancing maritime cooperation in the Indo-Pacific.
Supreme Court upholds mandate for complete digitization of land records, emphasizing transparency and reduced disputes.
ISRO successfully tests new generation semi-cryogenic engine for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program, advancing cost-effective space access.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'National Climate Resilience Mission (NCRM)', consider the following statements:
1. It is a flagship initiative under 'Viksit Bharat 2047'.
2. It focuses exclusively on urban infrastructure development for climate adaptation.
3. The mission encourages significant private sector participation.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options: (a) 1 only
(b) 1 and 3 only
(c) 2 and 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The NCRM is indeed a flagship initiative under 'Viksit Bharat 2047'. Statement 2 is incorrect: NCRM aims to integrate climate adaptation across 12 key sectors, including agriculture, water resources, urban development, and coastal management, not exclusively urban infrastructure. Statement 3 is correct: The mission explicitly encourages significant private sector participation through incentive schemes.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the current stance of the Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) regarding its policy rate?
options: (a) Accommodative, to boost economic growth.
(b) Withdrawal of accommodation, to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
(c) Neutral, to maintain a balance between growth and inflation.
(d) Tightening, to significantly reduce money supply.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Withdrawal of accommodation, to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The RBI Governor reiterated the commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the 4% target. This stance indicates that the central bank is focused on bringing inflation down, even if it means not actively supporting growth through rate cuts.

prelimsMocks.question: 'Varuna-2024' is a bilateral naval exercise conducted between India and which other country?
options: (a) United States
(b) Russia
(c) France
(d) Japan
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article states that 'Varuna-2024' is the 22nd edition of the bilateral naval exercise between the Indian Navy and the French Navy.

prelimsMocks.question: The Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP) is primarily aimed at:
options: (a) Promoting digital payments for agricultural produce.
(b) Digitizing land records to reduce disputes and enhance transparency.
(c) Providing digital literacy to rural farmers.
(d) Developing smart villages with digital infrastructure.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Digitizing land records to reduce disputes and enhance transparency.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court judgment highlighted that digitization of land records under DILRMP is crucial for reducing land disputes, preventing fraudulent transactions, and enhancing transparency in land administration.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program, a 'semi-cryogenic engine' is considered a critical component because it aims to:
options: (a) Utilize solid propellants for higher thrust.
(b) Reduce launch costs and turnaround time for space missions.
(c) Achieve single-stage-to-orbit capability with minimal fuel.
(d) Facilitate interplanetary missions with human crew.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Reduce launch costs and turnaround time for space missions.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article states that the semi-cryogenic engine is a critical component for India's ambition to develop cost-effective space access and the RLV-TD project aims to achieve two-stage-to-orbit capability, significantly reducing launch costs and turnaround time.

mainsMocks.question: The newly launched National Climate Resilience Mission (NCRM) reflects India's proactive approach towards climate change. Discuss the multi-sectoral strategy of NCRM and its potential impact on achieving the 'Viksit Bharat 2047' vision. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Disaster and disaster management.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NCRM and its 'Viksit Bharat' link. Elaborate on its multi-sectoral approach (agriculture, water, urban, coastal). Discuss how climate-proofing infrastructure and livelihoods contribute to sustainable development, economic stability, and poverty reduction, aligning with the long-term vision of a developed India by 2047. Highlight the role of technology and community participation.

mainsMocks.question: Despite significant progress, Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in India often face financial constraints hindering effective urban governance. In light of the new guidelines for enhanced financial autonomy of ULBs, critically analyze their potential to address these challenges and improve urban service delivery. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by acknowledging ULB financial challenges. Explain the new guidelines' focus on innovative revenue mechanisms (municipal bonds, property tax reforms, user charges) and capacity building. Discuss the potential benefits: reduced dependence on grants, improved infrastructure, better service delivery, and decentralized governance. Critically analyze challenges like political will, public acceptance of new taxes, implementation capacity, and equitable distribution of resources. Conclude on the need for sustained reforms and strong governance.

mainsMocks.question: India's ambition to become a global hub for medical tourism presents both opportunities and challenges. Examine the key provisions of the new national policy to boost medical tourism and evaluate its prospects in leveraging India's healthcare capabilities and traditional medicine systems. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life (Health).
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce medical tourism's potential. Detail policy provisions: infrastructure, quality, AYUSH integration, dedicated portal, visa processes, specialized zones. Discuss opportunities: foreign exchange, employment, global reputation, promotion of AYUSH. Highlight challenges: quality control, ethical concerns, competition, infrastructure gaps, and need for robust regulatory framework. Conclude on the importance of a holistic approach for sustainable growth.

quickBytes.text: The National Climate Resilience Mission (NCRM) is a flagship initiative under 'Viksit Bharat 2047'.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) maintained the repo rate at 6.50% for the sixth consecutive time.
The 'Varuna' naval exercise is a bilateral drill between India and France.
The Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP) aims to digitize land records.
ISRO successfully tested a new generation semi-cryogenic engine for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program.
The Ganga River Dolphin is India's National Aquatic Animal and an indicator species of river health.
Critical minerals are vital for electric vehicles, renewable energy, and defense industries.
The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, gave constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies.
India is the largest producer of millets globally, promoting them as 'Shree Anna'.
The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham.

GS1.headline: Draft National Policy on Elderly Care Released, Focuses on Holistic Well-being and Digital Inclusion
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment today released the draft National Policy on Elderly Care for public feedback.
• The policy aims to create an age-friendly society by ensuring financial security, healthcare, housing, and protection against abuse for senior citizens.
• A key focus is on leveraging digital technology to provide accessible services, including telemedicine, online grievance redressal, and digital literacy programs for the elderly.
• It proposes strengthening family support systems and community-based care, along with promoting active and healthy aging.
• The draft policy highlights the need for dedicated infrastructure and trained professionals to cater to the growing demographic of the elderly in India.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
Population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
GS1.source: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
prelimsPoints: Draft National Policy on Elderly Care released by Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment.
Aims for age-friendly society, financial security, healthcare, protection.
Focus on digital technology for services (telemedicine, online grievance).
Promotes family and community-based care.
Addresses growing elderly demographic in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the demographic challenges posed by an aging population in India and the necessity of a comprehensive elderly care policy.
Analyze how the proposed policy addresses the multi-dimensional needs of senior citizens, including health, financial security, and social inclusion.
Evaluate the role of digital technology in enhancing the accessibility and quality of services for the elderly as envisioned in the draft policy.
Critically examine the balance between institutional care and family/community-based support systems in the context of Indian societal values.
GS1.imageDescription: An elderly couple using a tablet device, symbolizing digital inclusion and connectivity for senior citizens.
GS1.trivia: As per the 2011 Census, the population of senior citizens in India was 10.38 crore, projected to increase significantly by 2050.
